execution_summary: Built a Python 3.10 Docker environment with numpy, pandas, statsmodels, and pyreadstat. The container initially failed to run due to a missing image tag; rebuilt with the expected tag and re-ran. Inspected and corrected the Python entrypoint by importing Sum contrasts and fixing a string literal syntax error. After human approval, executed the replication script on the SPSS dataset. The focal two-way Type III ANOVA on PASAverage (Culture x WritingCondition) completed without errors. The replication does not find a significant Culture x WritingCondition interaction on PASAverage; instead, there are robust culture main effects across multiple specifications. Outputs (ANOVA tables and cell means) were written in the container to /app/data. The process followed the preregistered plan: load data, construct PASAverage, run Type III ANOVAs, compute cell means, and conduct robustness checks. No major deviations except minor code fixes for compatibility.

code_executed.logs: Loaded dataset with shape: (477, 76). Focal ANOVA (PASAverage ~ Culture * WritingCondition) Type III completed; interaction p=0.291, Culture main effect p=1.22e-05, WritingCondition main effect p=0.834. Cell means saved. Additional analyses: PASAverage ~ PANASNetScore p=0.0289; PASAverage ~ PANASNetScore * WritingCondition interaction p=0.928; Bail (raw) interaction ns; Bail (outliers removed) Culture main p=0.018; Bail (log) Culture main p=8.95e-06. Simple effects within groups: EA p=0.578; East Asian p=0.334. Culture differences by condition: control p=0.0139; mortality p=0.0002497. Robustness (>=30 words) interaction p=0.141; No-low-outliers interaction p=0.410; Non-prolific interaction p=0.195; Prolific interaction p=0.344 (Culture main p=0.025); Non-religious interaction p=0.208.
code_executed.environment: Docker container based on python:3.10-slim with numpy>=1.26.0, pandas>=2.0.0, statsmodels>=0.14.0, pyreadstat>=1.2.3, patsy

results.hypothesis_tested: Culture (European American vs. East Asian/Asian American) will interact with WritingCondition (mortality salience vs. control) on PASAverage: after mortality salience, Asian American participants will show higher PAS scores and European American participants will show lower PAS scores relative to control.

findings_summary.outcome_name: Culture x WritingCondition interaction on PASAverage (Type III ANOVA)
Main effect of Culture on PASAverage
findings_summary.direction: Culture groups differ in PASAverage regardless of condition

Simple effect of WritingCondition within European Americans
Simple effect of WritingCondition within East Asians

findings_summary.direction: Positive association: higher positive mood relates to higher PAS
